evals.prompt: What version will this release? I just merged a feat: commit adding the core/polling submodule to main.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs 'npx semantic-release@latest --dry-run --no-ci', reports the next version as a minor bump (0.x.0 → 0.x+1.0), shows the release notes grouped by commit type, and explains that feat: triggers a minor.
expectations: Runs 'git fetch' to confirm the branch is up to date
Reports a minor version bump (feat: → minor)
Shows the release notes or a summary of what semantic-release computed

evals.prompt: Preview the release — I only merged a fix: commit correcting a missing .js extension in the core barrel.
evals.expected_output: Skill reports a patch bump (0.x.y → 0.x.y+1) and the release notes showing the fix: commit. Explains that fix: triggers a patch.
expectations: Mentions that fix: commits trigger a patch release

evals.prompt: Will this release? I only merged docs: and chore: commits.
evals.expected_output: Skill reports 'no release will be published' and explains that docs:, chore:, refactor:, and test: commits do not trigger semantic-release.
expectations: Reports that no release will be published
Explains which commit types do not trigger a release (docs:, chore:, refactor:, test:)

evals.prompt: Did my commits bump the version correctly? I added a feat!: commit renaming M3LPaths.outputDir to archiveDir.
evals.expected_output: Skill reports a major version bump (x.y.z → x+1.0.0) and shows the BREAKING CHANGE note in the release notes. Confirms feat!: triggers a major.
expectations: Shows or references the BREAKING CHANGE footer in the release notes
Explains that feat!: or a BREAKING CHANGE footer triggers a major release
